text: Nuclear-powered vacuum cleaners will probably be a reality within ten years.
author: Alex Lewyt

didItHoldUp.analysis: The president of a vacuum company looked at the atomic age and thought: why not put a reactor in a Hoover? He wasn't alone — the 1950s imagined nuclear-powered everything, from cars to airplanes. The physics told a different story. Reactor shielding alone would weigh tons, and the radiation risk of a household nuclear device is, to put it mildly, a dealbreaker. We got cordless vacuums powered by lithium batteries instead — less dramatic, but nobody needs a hazmat suit to clean the living room.
